rationale.R1.2: Verifying evidence-claim alignment requires auditing the logical chain from reported numbers to stated conclusions. The GM argument contains a critical inversion: a gain margin of 8.4 dB is larger than 2.1 dB, meaning the open-loop baseline actually tolerates more additional loop gain before instability — the standard engineering interpretation of a higher GM is greater, not lesser, stability margin. The paper's gloss ('ControlRoute closes the loop with greater authority and consequently realizes superior closed-loop stability') inverts this relationship, making logical consistency load-bearing for this delegation.
rationale.R1.3: This is the direct object of the delegation. Must check whether (i) e_ss = 0.03 vs 0.07 genuinely establishes robust tracking under perturbation (single held-out week, no confidence intervals or statistical tests reported, no characterization of perturbation magnitude d(k)), and whether (ii) the GM comparison is interpreted correctly and supports the stability-superiority claim given the directional inversion identified under R1.2.
